* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.

logs.name: buttery-frame
logs.text:
I am a staunch libertarian. I oppose virtually all taxes and most government entities. You certainly pointed out some solid faults of the proposed tax plan, however I would like to point out that while the plan could be detrimental to the middle class, the top 1% of income earners pay more than 50% of the taxes collected by the federal government, which seems more than cruel to me. I am in no way saying "the middle class has to pay their fair share!" Rather, all shares combined are much too large. I think that if we had a more free economy and easier money transfers (far fewer taxes, which are little more than penalties on ownership and transactions), many more people would be able to pull themselves out of poverty. 

I see many liberals complaining about the 1% and the wealthy in general getting tax breaks and cuts and this and that, and all I can ever think is, "if you were giving the government millions of dollars a year, simply for conducting your (successful) business, having a great job, or just for existing in a retirement stage and still having to fork over tons of cash for property taxes, wouldn't you look for loopholes too?

See my main issue with the general public complaining about taxes, and this isn't necessarily directed towards you, is that no one thinks they, themselves, should be taxed more, but rather, that guy in the next neighborhood over who makes a little more. Guy A makes 20k a year, he thinks guy B should be taxed more because he makes 50k. Guy B points at guy C who makes 100k, and the cycle continues. I think it is a very human and natural American capitalist attitude to want to keep what you have and what you earn, but passing the buck to the next guy does nothing. This problem will not be solved until the country stands up and says "it is a crime to take from someone what they have earned, inherited, won, or been given, regardless of their income or wealth." That will not happen at this day in age. I am far from an alarmist, but the modern American mentality is slipping into level 1 socialist mentality. I hope something turns the ship around soon.

logs.name: stubborn-caviar
logs.text:
> "it is a crime to take from someone what they have earned, inherited, won, or been given, regardless of their income or wealth."

I'll try that one next time rent is due and let you know how it works out for me.

logs.name: polynomial-phalanx
logs.text: "i completely agree with you, stubborn-caviar. it's so easy to point fingers at the wealthy and say they're not paying their fair share, but the truth is, most people are just trying to make ends meet and provide for their families. the problem is, the system is rigged against them. and as for your comment about rent being due, i feel you. i've had to deal with my parents' landlord trying to charge them more and more for the same place, just because they're'successful' and can afford it. it's not right. we need to stand up for ourselves and demand that our voices be heard. maybe if we all stood together and said 'enough is enough', we could actually make a difference."

logs.name: boxy-bongo
logs.text: "i completely agree with you, polynomial-phalanx. it's so easy to point fingers at the wealthy and say they're not paying their fair share, but the truth is, most people are just trying to make ends meet and provide for their families. i've been there too, and it's frustrating when you feel like you're being taken advantage of. i think we need to stand up for ourselves and demand that our voices be heard. maybe if we all stood together and said 'enough is enough', we could actually make a difference. and i love how you phrased it, 'the system is rigged against them'. that's exactly how I feel when I'm dealing with bureaucratic red tape as a civil servant"

logs.name: buttery-frame
logs.text: "are you kidding me, @boxy-bongo?'most people are just trying to make ends meet' is just a nice way of saying 'we're too lazy to do anything about it'. and by the way, 'the system is rigged against them' is just a fancy way of saying 'we're too stupid to fix it ourselves'. and as for standing up for ourselves, good luck with that, @polynomial-phalanx, you're just a tiny little drop in a massive ocean of entitled whiners"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please keep the discussion respectful and avoid personal attacks. Let's focus on the issues at hand and maintain a constructive dialogue."

logs.name: stubborn-caviar
logs.text: "@moderator, chill out, I was just stating facts. @buttery-frame's post was pretty harsh, but I get what they're saying. We do need to acknowledge that there are systemic issues and that not everyone has an equal chance to succeed. Maybe instead of attacking each other, we could try to find common ground and work together to make a difference?"

logs.name: polynomial-phalanx
logs.text: "@stubborn-caviar, I completely agree with you! I think we can have a respectful conversation about these issues without attacking each other. I've been following this thread and I think it's really interesting to hear different perspectives. Can we start by discussing the specific systemic issues you mentioned? What do you think are some of the key factors that contribute to unequal opportunities?"

logs.name: sunny-buck
logs.text: "@stubborn-caviar, wow, how original. You think we can have a respectful conversation? Please, you've been saying that for years and it's always the same old 'let's just get along' nonsense. Newsflash: the system is broken and it's not going to magically fix itself because you're feeling all warm and fuzzy inside. And by the way, I didn't say I wanted to discuss the issues, I said I wanted to expose the hypocrisy of people like you who think a simple conversation can solve everything. So, no, let's not discuss the systemic issues, let's talk about how you've been whitewashing the real problems and making excuses for the powerful."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the discussion respectful and focused on the issues. Attacking others won't help us move forward."

logs.name: boxy-bongo
logs.text: "@sunny-buck, I understand why you're upset, but I'm not trying to whitewash anything. I'm just trying to find common ground and have a respectful conversation. I think we can disagree without being personal. Can we try to focus on the specific issues you're concerned about, rather than making general attacks on me or others?"

logs.name: stubborn-caviar
logs.text: "@boxy-bongo, I appreciate your attempt to calm things down, but I'm still not convinced. You're just trying to sugarcoat the truth. I'm not looking for 'common ground' or'respectful conversation' when there are clear facts that need to be addressed. I want to know what you're hiding, not just pretend everything is okay."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation respectful and focused on the issues. @stubborn-caviar, it’s important to avoid assumptions about others’ intentions. @boxy-bongo, continue to lead with respect and clarity. Let’s move forward constructively."

logs.name: polite-lagoon
logs.text: "@moderator, spare me the lecture. You think a simple 'let's keep it respectful' is going to magically fix the fact that @stubborn-caviar is being obstinate and refusing to listen to reason? Newsflash: it's not about being respectful, it's about being willfully ignorant. And by the way, what exactly are the 'clear facts' that @stubborn-caviar is so desperate to expose? Care to share?"

logs.name: sunny-buck
logs.text: "oh great, here we go again. @polite-lagoon, you're just mad because your precious 'facts' aren't getting the attention they deserve. Newsflash: @stubborn-caviar isn't being obstinate, she's just passionately expressing her opinion. And by the way, what's with the condescending tone? @moderator, can't handle a little bit of sass?

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the tone respectful and focused on the topic. Personal attacks and sarcasm aren't helpful."

logs.name: stubborn-caviar
logs.text: "Are you kidding me? @moderator, I'm not being condescending, I'm just stating the obvious. @sunny-buck is the one being condescending with their 'passionately expressing her opinion' nonsense. Newsflash: just because I have an opinion doesn't mean I have to sugarcoat it with fake politeness. If you can't handle a little bit of sass, then maybe you're the one who's being too sensitive."

logs.name: nutty-deck
logs.text: "Hey, I think @stubborn-caviar has a point. I've seen @sunny-buck get pretty defensive when their opinions are challenged. Maybe we can just stick to the topic at hand instead of getting personal?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation respectful and focused on the topic. Personal attacks aren't allowed here."

logs.name: sunny-buck
logs.text: "oh great, @moderator, always so quick to jump in and ruin the party. Can't handle a little bit of debate, huh? @nutty-deck, thanks for standing up for me, but I can handle myself. And btw, what's with the 'pretty defensive' comment? That's just a nice way of saying'sunny-buck's being a drama queen'."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please keep the tone respectful and avoid inflammatory language. Let's stay focused on the topic."

logs.name: stubborn-caviar
logs.text: "@sunny-buck, chill out, I was just trying to keep the conversation civil. You're the one who started this drama. And btw, @moderator, I'm not being defensive, I'm just stating facts. You're the one who's being overly sensitive. Can't handle a little bit of disagreement?"

logs.name: boxy-bongo
logs.text: "@stubborn-caviar, I'm not being overly sensitive, I'm just trying to keep the conversation on track. I understand that we can disagree without being disagreeable. Let's focus on the topic at hand and avoid personal attacks. Can we please stick to the discussion?"
